Where children learn
from the land

Nestled in the canyons of Silverado since 1990, SCC offers an emergent, nature-based curriculum where kids get muddy, climb trees, care for animals, and grow.

Canyon Hikes

California state law requires all enrolled children to be fully vaccinated.  View our vaccine requirements →

Our Philosophy

A classroom without walls

At Silverado Children's Center, the canyon is the curriculum. Children lace up their shoes every morning for a hike — along Bobcat Creek, up to the Hawk Tree, or through the trails winding behind the school. Afternoons bring the animal yard, the garden, and the mud kitchen. We believe deep learning happens when children are given time, space, and real things to explore.

“An appreciation of nature's handiwork, care for living creatures, and a solid respect for the environment.”

What We Offer

Three ways children thrive here

🌿

Nature Play

Every day starts with a hike. Bobcat Creek, the Hawk Tree, the Stables — SCC’s canyon backyard is a living classroom. Back at school, tire swings, mud kitchens, and open hillside keep the adventure going.

🎨

Hands-On Learning

Finger painting, making their own snacks, mud play, gardening — we believe in learning through doing, making, and discovering with all the senses.

🐐

Animal Care

SCC is home to a real animal family — resident creatures that kids help care for. It's one of the things that makes this place unlike any other preschool.

Our Animal Family

Not many preschools have goats.

SCC is home to two goats, a miniature horse, a tortoise, a leopard gecko, and the wild bunnies that roam the hills beyond the fence. Kids help care for the animals, take the tortoise for rides in a red wagon, and keep an eye on the wildlife camera pointed at the canyon — you never know what turns up overnight.



Meet the Animals

Join Our Community

Ready to see it for yourself?

The best way to understand SCC is to visit. Come see the canyon, meet the animals, and talk with our teachers. Enrollment opens after your tour.
